reasons: Estes extends Thamuz's frontline uptime, letting Thamuz stay inside long fights.
Thamuz naturally wants repeated trades, so sustained healing directly supports his fight pattern.

proof.summary: Thamuz wants to remain in melee range for extended damage, while Estes helps keep him alive through repeated healing.
worksBestWhen: Estes stays close enough to maintain healing links.
Thamuz fights within team range instead of chasing too far alone.
failureCases: Thamuz overextends outside Estes's support range.
Enemy anti-heal and burst damage cut through the sustain plan.

reasons: Angela can attach to Thamuz when he commits, giving him shield and support during deep frontline trades.
Angela's movement and slow support help Thamuz stay close to targets during extended fights.

proof.summary: Angela adds protection to Thamuz's commit window, making his extended brawl harder to punish.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches as Thamuz starts the real engage.
Thamuz uses the support to pressure priority targets, not only tanks.
failureCases: Angela attaches after Thamuz has already lost too much HP.
Enemy disengage prevents Thamuz from staying in melee range.

reasons: Floryn can support Thamuz during side-lane or jungle skirmishes without needing to stand beside him.
Global healing gives Thamuz another sustain layer when he commits into long fights.

proof.summary: Thamuz can fight far from the backline, and Floryn's global heal helps cover that distance problem.
worksBestWhen: Floryn times Bloom for Thamuz's all-in or low-health brawl.
Thamuz uses the heal to reset the fight instead of chasing into isolated danger.
failureCases: Thamuz is controlled and burst before the heal can change the fight.

reasons: Hylos's path can speed up Thamuz's engage and slow enemies, helping Thamuz stay close enough to fight.
Double frontline pressure creates space for Thamuz to brawl without being isolated.

proof.summary: Hylos improves Thamuz's approach and chase pattern, making Thamuz's sustained melee pressure easier to apply.
worksBestWhen: Hylos places the path toward the fight or objective.
Thamuz follows the path timing instead of entering before the team is ready.
failureCases: Enemy disengage avoids the path direction.
Hylos and Thamuz both commit without backline follow-up.

reasons: Faramis lets Thamuz commit harder in teamfights because Nether Realm gives extra survival value.
Thamuz can keep pressuring enemies during the protection window instead of immediately retreating.

proof.summary: Thamuz wants to stay inside the fight, and Faramis can protect that commit with teamfight survival utility.
worksBestWhen: Faramis uses Nether Realm before Thamuz is finished by burst.
Thamuz keeps fighting near teammates who can also use the protection window.
failureCases: Thamuz dives outside Nether Realm value.
Enemy waits out the protection window before re-engaging.
